codeReview.summary: Reviewed mission artifacts, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit eb65d2ce. The layout/navigation restyle is mostly aligned with the feature scope, but breadcrumb coloring has a requirement-breaking logic issue.

issues.description: Breadcrumb rendering treats any crumb without href as active (`var(--fg)`) via `isLast || !crumb.href`, so non-last crumbs without href are not muted. This violates the expected behavior "Last item --fg, others --fg-muted". Reproducible with current breadcrumb definitions on pages that provide multi-item trails without href on the first item (e.g., ui/src/pages/InstanceSettings.tsx:35-38, ui/src/pages/InstanceGeneralSettings.tsx:15-18, ui/src/pages/InstanceExperimentalSettings.tsx:15-18).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The gui-worker skill's startup command is out of sync with the repository services manifest.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/gui-worker/SKILL.md step 5 instructs `pnpm dev`, while .factory/services.yaml defines `api.start` as `PORT=3100 pnpm dev:once`; transcript skeleton for session 5d18f4ab-b96e-4ea8-a697-3d1f3e44bca2 shows the worker used `PORT=3100 pnpm dev:once`.

summary: Scrutiny result: fail. A blocking breadcrumb styling logic bug remains for non-last crumbs without href; otherwise the reviewed feature changes are scoped and largely consistent with the intended GUI polish.
